epigraph.quote: I had the best night's sleep ever. Best view ever.

per_stop.quote: Jenny gave us lovely pasta.
The supper at 8 (booked head) was delicious. Rump of Kent lamb + cod Stalisfield Green.
There's a Fair in the square. But I don't want to go on the rides. I must be getting old.
Everything shut up, a free night. But oh so cold.
Past Lucerne. Didn't see Bridge.
A room facing village was 220, but we faced lake (240€) which had a glorious view all evening + all night.
We had coffee, bought cake & cycled back to the van.
In the evening we had supper next to an Italian couple who now run a 22 bed guest house.
Such wonderful views in a full 180°, & just over the ridge there are views the other way. Mile after mile. 100 miles. Sunshine.
As we left we saw Anna & boyfriend loading her dog onto the back of the motor bike.
It was a shock to see soldiers with machine guns at the Basilica
The Piero della Francesca altarpiece was magnificent. I could have sat looking at it for ours.
An odd bird here - horizontal black + white stripes + a red hat
we saw the gorgeous Fra Angelico Annunciation.
The colours in Christ's passion are fabulous + fresh
At Piacenza we stopped to visit a church in the style of Bramante but it was closed.
We have been drinking sweet asti spumante for supper.
French bread. Bugs, Surfs, sunset, sunshine.
The monks were invited in 1860, to drain the lakes because the average age was only 20-30.
We have waited 25 years to have supper in the St. Jean de Vaux restaurant, but it has always been closed in August.
We had to move the van into the middle of the square because of the market the next day.
So many towns & villages have lost their bakeries.
